content.full_text:
STATE OF CAI.IFORNIA 
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
1102 Q Street• Suite 3000 • Sacramento, CA 95811 
(1)1'>) :::2-6(10 • rax (916) ;l:!:.?-11886 
Nathan Magsig 
Fresno County Supervisor 
2424 Sample A venue 
Clovis, CA 93611 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-17-202 
Dear Mr. Magsig: 
October 20, 2017 
This letter responds to your request for advice regarding the conflict of interest provisions of 
the Political Reform Act (the "Act") 1 and Section 1090. Please note that we are only providing 
advice under the conflict of interest provisions of the Act and Section 1090, and we offer no 
opinion on the applicability of other conflict of interest laws, such as the conflict of interest 
provisions of the Public Contract Code or common law conflicts of interest. We are also not a 
finder of fact when rendering advice (In re Oglesby (1975) 1 FPPC Ops. 71 ), and any advice we 
provide assumes your facts are complete and accurate. 
In regard to our advice on Section 1090, we are required to forward your request and all 
pertinent facts relating to the request to the Attorney General's Office and the Fresno County 
District Attorney's Office, which we have done. (Section 1097.l(c)(3).) We did not receive a 
written response from either entity. (Section 1097. l(c)(4).) We are also required to advise you that, 
for purposes of Section 1090, the following advice "is not admissible in a criminal proceeding 
against any individual other than the requestor." (See Section 1097. l(c)(S).) 
Because your question regarding the Act is general in nature and not limited to a specific 
governmental decision, we only provide informal assistance2 with respect to that question. 
QUESTIONS 
1. Does Section 1090 prohibit you from entering into a contract with Schneider Electric 
Buildings Americas, Inc. ("Schneider Electric") under which you would provide consulting services 
as an independent contractor regarding potential water, waste-water, or energy projects in various 
cities throughout the San Joaquin Valley, including cities within Fresno County? 
1 The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory 
references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices 
Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code ofRegulations. All 
regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated. 
2 lnfonnal assistance does not provide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or formal 
written advice. (Section 83114; Regulation l 8329(c)(3).) 


File No. 1-17-202 
Page No. 2 
2. If you enter into the consulting services contract with Schneider Electric, would the Act 
prohibit you from talcing part in governmental decisions relating to your consulting services 
business, Schneider Electric, or your personal finances? 
CONCLUSIONS 
1. No. Section 1090 does not prohibit you from entering into the consulting services contract 
with Schneider Electric in your private capacity. However, if you enter into that contract, Section 
I 090 may prohibit you from making or participating in the making of, or Fresno County from 
entering into, a future contract with Schneider Electric. Accordingly, if you enter into that contract, 
you should seek further advice prior to making or participating in the making of, or the County 
entering into, a future contract with Schneider Electric. 
2. The Act would prohibit you from taking part in a decision if that decision would have a 
reasonably foreseeable material financial effect on your consulting services business, Schneider 
Electric, or your personal finances. 
FACTS 
You are a member of the Fresno County Board of Supervisors. Schneider Electric has asked 
you to provide consulting services as an independent contractor regarding potential water, waste­
water, or energy projects in various cities throughout the San Joaquin Valley, including cities within 
Fresno County. Under the proposed contract, your consulting business would be charged with 
generating projects in expressly targeted cities, introducing Schneider Electric to executive-level 
decision makers, and providing assistance to Schneider Electric on its existing projects. And 
Schneider Electric would pay your consulting services business $2,000 per month plus expenses 
under the proposed contract. The proposed contract expressly targets 16 cities within the San 
Joaquin Valley, including 6 cities within Fresno County. However, the County is not a party to the 
proposed contract, and the contract makes no reference to the County except to note that your 
consulting business would be responsible for compliance with federal, State, county or local laws. 
ANALYSIS 
Section 1090 
Section 1090 provides, in pertinent part, that "[m]embers of the Legislature, state, county, 
district, judicial district, and city officers or employees shall not be financially interested in any 
contract made by them in their official capacity, or by any body or board of which they are 
members." 
As the California Supreme Court has stated, the purpose of Section 1090 is to make certain 
that "every public officer be guided solely by the public interest, rather than by personal interest, 
when dealing with contracts in an official capacity." (Thomson v. Call (1985) 38 Cal.3d 633, 650.) 
A contract that violates Section 1090 is void, and the prohibition applies regardless of whether the 
terms of the contract are fair and equitable to all parties. (Id. at pp. 646-649.) 


File No. 1-17-202 
Page No. 3 
Section 1090 would not prohibit you from entering into the consulting services contract with 
Schneider Electric because you would not enter into that contract in your official capacity. Under 
that contract, you would serve as an independent contractor for Schneider Electric in your private 
capacity. The County is not a party to that contract, and the only reference to the County in the 
contract is the requirement that your consulting services business comply with federal, State, 
county, and local laws. Based on these facts, Section 1090 would not prohibit you from making, 
participating in the making of, or entering into that contract. 
However, if you enter into the consulting services contract with Schneider Electric, you may 
become financially interested in Schneider Electric as a result, and therefore, Section 1090 may 
prohibit you from making or participating in the making of, or Fresno County from entering into, a 
future contract with Schneider Electric. Accordingly, if you enter into the proposed contract, you 
should seek further advice prior to making or participating in the making of, or the County entering 
into, a future contract with Schneider Electric. 
The Act 
Section 87100 of the Act prohibits a public official from making, participating in the 
making, or otherwise using his or her official position to influence a governmental decision in 
which the official has a financial interest. Section 87103 provides that a public official has a 
"financial interest" in a decision, within the meaning of the Act, if it is reasonably foreseeable that 
the decision will have a material financial effect on one or more of the official's interests identified 
in that section. Section 87103 identifies the following financial interests: 
• Any business entity in which the official has a direct or indirect investment worth $2,000 or 
more (Section 87103(a)), or in which the official is a director, officer, partner, trustee, 
employee, or holds any position of management (Section 87103(d)). 
• Any real property in which the official has a direct or indirect interest worth $2,000 or more. 
(Section 87103(b).) 
• Any source of income, except gifts or loans by a commercial lending institution made in the 
regular course of business on terms available to the public without regard to official status, 
aggregating $500 or more in value provided or promised to, received by, the official within 12 
months prior to the decision.3 (Section 87103(c).) 
• Any donor of, or intermediary or agent for a donor of, a gift or gifts aggregating $470 or more 
in value provided to, received by, or promised to the official within 12 months prior to the 
decision. (Section 87103{e).) 
• An interest in his or her personal finances and those of immediate family members. (See Section 
87103.) 
3 Section 82030 sets forth the Act's definition of"income," and provides that income "of an individual also 
includes a pro rata share of any income of any business entity or trust in which the individual or spouse owns, directly, 
indirectly or beneficially, a to-percent interest or greater." 


File No. l-17-202 
Page No. 4 
Therefore, if you enter into the consulting services contract with Schneider Electric, you 
would have a business interest in your consulting services business and a source-of-income interest 
in Schneider Electric. And a public official always has an interest in his or her personal finances. 
Foreseeability and Materiality 
A decision's financial effect on an official's financial interest is presumed to be reasonably 
foreseeable if the interest is a named party in, or the subject of, the decision. (Regulation 1870l(a).) 
An interest is the subject of a proceeding if the decision involves the issuance, renewal, approval, 
denial, or revocation of any license, permit, or other entitlement to, or contract with, the interest. 
(Ibid.) lf the interest is not a named party in or the subject of the decision, the decision's financial 
effect is reasonably foreseeable if it can be recognized as a realistic possibility and more than 
hypothetical or theoretical. (Regulation 18701 (b ). ) 
Whether a financial effect will be material depends on the type of interest affected by the 
decision. In this case, the materiality standards for a business entity, such as your consulting 
services business, and a source of income in a business entity, such as Schneider Electric, are 
provided in Regulation 18702.1. The materially standards for your interest in your personal finances 
is set forth in Regulation 18702.5.4 
Because you have not asked for assistance regarding a specific government decision, we can 
only generally advise that the Act would prohibit you from taking part in a decision if that decision 
would have a reasonably foreseeable material financial effect on your consulting services business, 
Schneider Electric, or your personal finances. lf you need assistance determining whether a specific 
decision may have a reasonably foreseeable material financial effect on one or more of your 
interests, you should seek further advice identifying the specific decision at issue. 
lf you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660. 
Sincerely, 
Brian G. Lau 
Assistant General Counsel 
 
By: 
Matthew F. Christy 
Counsel, Legal Division 
MFC:jgl 
4 However, under Regulation 18702.S(c), an effect on your personal finances is not analyzed separately if the 
decision affects an interest in a business entity.
